title: Lathyrus maritimus
taxonomy: Lathyrus maritimus is a species of flowering plant in the pea family (Fabaceae) and is commonly known as the sea pea or beach pea. It is native to Europe and Western Asia, but its exact classification can be further specified into the subfamily Faboideae and tribe Loteae. This classification reflects the plant's evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other members of the pea family. The plant's classification also indicates its close affinity with other legumes, which are nitrogen-fixing plants that play a crucial role in maintaining soil fertility. Further classification can be done at the species level, but Lathyrus maritimus is widely recognized as a distinct species within the pea family.
uses: Lathyrus maritimus, also known as sea pea or beach pea, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its leaves and stems are said to have anti-inflammatory properties and may be used to treat minor injuries and skin irritations. The plant is also believed to have antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent infections. In some cultures, the seeds of Lathyrus maritimus are used as a natural remedy for digestive issues, such as diarrhea and constipation. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Lathyrus maritimus for medicinal purposes.

The plant is also believed to have antimicrobial properties, which could aid in the treatment of infections and wounds. preparations: Lathyrus maritimus, also known as sea pea, has been used in herbal preparations for its potential medicinal properties. side_effects: Lathyrus maritimus, also known as sea pea, contains a toxin called ODAP or oxalyldiaminopropionic acid, which can cause neurological problems and other side effects if consumed in large amounts. Eating sea pea seeds regularly can lead to a condition called neurolathyrism, characterized by paralysis, muscle spasms, and other neurological symptoms. Prolonged consumption of the seeds can also cause birth defects and reproductive issues in animals. The exact dose of ODAP that causes adverse effects in humans is unknown. Consuming Lathyrus maritimus seeds in moderation may be safe, but it's essential to consult with a medical professional or a qualified botanist for guidance.
intro: Lathyrus maritimus, also known as sea pea, is a perennial legume native to the coastal regions of Europe and North Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms of gastrointestinal disorders, such as diarrhea and dyspepsia, due to its potential health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Lathyrus maritimus include isoleucine, a non-protein amino acid, as well as other compounds like alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, sea pea is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in combination with other herbs in various remedies.
